items.title: USMNT injury scare as Dest 'out for a little while'
items.summary: U.S. defender Sergiño Dest limped off with an apparent hamstring injury during PSV Eindhoven's 2-1 win over AZ Alkmaar in the Dutch league on Saturday.

items.title: Messi paid $70M-$80M per year, Miami owner says
items.summary: Inter Miami co-owner Jorge Mas revealed that captain Lionel Messi is paid between $70 million and $80 million a year, including the player's ownership shares.

items.title: Police call Celtic-Rangers fan trouble 'shameful'
items.summary: Police stated they faced "extreme hostility and violence" during a post-match pitch invasion at Ibrox which impacted Celtic staff and players.

items.title: Lloris has 4th clean sheet in LAFC's perfect start
items.summary: David Martínez scored in the second half and Hugo Lloris made it stand up for a third straight clean sheet to begin the season as LAFC beat FC Dallas 1-0 on Saturday night.

items.title: Cruijff krijgt de sleutels van de kluis bij Ajax: 'Maar niet als Mislintat'
items.summary: Het besluit om Fred Grim te vervangen als interim-coach van Ajax , zorgt voor gemengde gevoelens aan tafel bij Studio Voetbal.

items.title: VI Live: Milan verdedigt nipte voorsprong in Derby della Madonnina
items.summary: Zondag staan er vijf wedstrijden in de Eredivisie op het programma. Het duel van Feyenoord volgen we in een apart liveblog. Verder zijn de verrichtingen op de buitenlandse velden in deze VI Live te volgen, bijvoorbeeld in de derby tussen AC Milan en Internazionale.

items.title: Pak Schaal Podcast Extra: Spaanse enclave moet slap Ajax tot leven wekken
items.summary: Jordi Cruijff heeft ingegrepen bij Ajax. Fred Grim is niet langer meer de interim-trainer van de Amsterdammers. Vanaf morgen staat Oscar Garcia met zijn Spaanse assistent-trainers voor de groep. In een Pak Schaal Podcast Extra bespreken Freek Jansen en Arco Gnocchi de volgende trainerswissel in de hoofdstad.

items.title: NEC wervelt door en hijgt Feyenoord weer in de nek na klinkende zege op Volendam
items.summary: NEC heeft zondagavond afgerekend met FC Volendam. De ploeg van Dick Schreuder loopt daarmee in op Feyenoord. Het gat naar de tweede plek – goed voor een direct Champions League-ticket – is nu nog maar drie punten. Gezien beide ploegen elkaar over een maand treffen, heeft NEC het in eigen hand.

items.title: Henk de Jong durft het aan en spreekt zich uit: 'Dat gaat gebeuren'
items.summary: SC Cambuur profiteerde zondag van het eerdere (punten)verlies van concurrenten ADO Den Haag, De Graafschap, Willem II en Roda JC. De Leeuwarders staan daardoor wat verliespunten betreft bovenaan de Keuken Kampioen Divisie en promotie kan vrijwel niet meer misgaan. Henk de Jong spreekt het voor het eerst hardop uit. 'Dat gaat gebeuren', zei hij over promotie.

items.title: 'Shameful' pitch invasion by Celtic and Rangers fans condemned by police
items.summary: Arrests were made with both police officers and fans injured during clashes following Celtic's 4-2 win on penalties.

items.title: Police condemn ‘shameful’ behaviour of Celtic and Rangers fans in Ibrox clashes
items.summary: Police say arrests have been made after Scottish Cup tie ‘Officers and stewards faced with hostility and violence’ Police Scotland have condemned as “shameful” the behaviour of some supporters and said arrests have been made after clashes at the end of at the Scottish Cup quarter-final between Rangers and Celtic at Ibrox. Chief Superintendent Kate Stephen said: “The behaviour of a number of supporters at the Scottish Cup quarter-final between Rangers and Celtic at Ibrox today was shameful. It must be condemned by everyone involved in football and wider society. Continue reading...

items.title: Leeds power past Norwich to reach FA Cup last eight for first time since 2003
items.summary: After Southampton and Port Vale caused shocks, could Philippe Clement mastermind a third of the day for Norwich? The answer was a resounding no as Leeds United cuffed their guests aside and progressed to a first quarter-final in 23 years. Leeds have not contested a semi‑final since 1987 when losing 3-2 against Coventry after extra time but are only 90 minutes away from breaching a 53-year gap to the pre­vious walk-out at Wembley in the competition: the 1973 showpiece, which Sunderland won 1-0. Continue reading...

items.title: New Zealand's hearts broken again as India add to white-ball dynasty
items.summary: "I wouldn't mind breaking a few hearts," said New Zealand captain Mitchell Santner on the eve of the T20 World Cup final against India in their opponents' homeland.

items.title: League One side Port Vale stun Sunderland in FA Cup upset
items.summary: League One side Port Vale knock out Premier League team Sunderland to reach the FA Cup quarter-finals for the first time since 1954.

items.title: Boro thrash QPR to boost automatic promotion hopes
items.summary: Middlesbrough emphatically boosted their automatic promotion hopes by thrashing QPR 4-0 at Loftus Road.

items.title: Leeds power past Norwich to reach first FA Cup quarter-final since 2003
items.summary: Leeds reached their first FA Cup quarter-final since 2003 after easing past Championship side Norwich with a 3-0 victory at Elland Road.

items.title: 'Disgraceful scenes as toxicity spills over in Old Firm cup tie'
items.summary: As if the ugliness of the 120 minutes wasn't enough there was disgrace as Rangers and Celtic fans invaded the pitch at Ibrox, writes Tom English.

items.title: Sivo hat-trick takes Leeds to Castleford victory as Hull KR bounce back
items.summary: Maika Sivo scored a hat-trick as Leeds claimed a 22-4 Betfred Super League win over Castleford.

items.title: Bottom of the league, magic in the cup - Port Vale's 'incredible' run
items.summary: Port Vale are bottom of League One, but they have reached the FA Cup quarter-finals for only the second time in their history.

items.title: India hammer New Zealand in final to make T20 World Cup history
items.summary: India became the first men's side to win the T20 World Cup three times and the first to claim back-to-back titles with an emphatic 96-run victory over New Zealand continuing their white-ball dominance.

items.title: Predicting the USWNT's starting lineup for next year's World Cup
items.summary: With the 2027 Women's World Cup closer than you think, we look at the USWNT's roster and forecast the 11 players Emma Hayes will count on for the tournament.

items.title: Oxford United academy player, 15, dies after collapsing during match
items.summary: Amelia Aplin collapsed in fixture against Fulham Clubs and Football Association pay tribute to player A 15-year-old Oxford United academy player has died after collapsing during a match, the club has confirmed in a statement expressing “immense sadness” at the news. Amelia Aplin collapsed on Saturday during the girls’ academy fixture against Fulham at Oxford’s Horspath training centre and later the club said a “serious medical incident” had occurred. Continue reading...

items.title: Port Vale stun Sunderland to reach first FA Cup quarter-final in 72 years
items.summary: Ben Waine, a Newcastle fan from New Zealand, reproduced Alan Shearer’s trademark single-arm cele­bration in front of the travelling ­Sunderland fans after scoring the goal that knocked the Premier League side out of the FA Cup as Port Vale, bottom of League One, reached the quarter-finals of this competition for the first time in 72 years. The Newcastle legend did not hesitate to retweet the BBC’s post as Waine, who scored the fourth-round winner against Bristol City in the delayed tie five days earlier, helped Jon Brady’s side to make light of the 56 league places between them and Sunderland. Waine also scored the second‑round winner against Bristol Rovers as Vale, 11 points adrift of the safety line in League One, have found solace in the Cup. Indeed their five Cup triumphs are only one short of their number of league wins. Continue reading...

items.title: Southampton knock out Fulham with stoppage-time penalty
items.summary: Southampton's Ross Stewart scores a penalty in stoppage time against Fulham which sends the Championship side to the FA Cup quarter-finals.

items.title: Southampton into last eight after Ross Stewart’s late penalty upsets Fulham
items.summary: The winning goal arrived late but the result was deserved. ­Southampton were resilient in defence, comba­tive in midfield, dangerous on the counterattack and are into the quarter-finals of the FA Cup after punishing another dozy home performance from ­Fulham with a stoppage-time penalty from Ross Stewart. As wild as the celebrations were in the away end, it did not feel like much of a shock. Having recovered from last season’s limp relegation from the Premier League, Southampton were good value for the win. Continue reading...

items.title: Met vertrouwen naar Genk: zo kon STVV Cercle Brugge kraken
items.summary: STVV trekt met vertrouwen naar KRC Genk. De Limburgers wonnen zondagavond met 2-1 van Cercle Brugge en komen zo weer op gelijke hoogte met Club Brugge.

items.title: 🎥 Deux buts dans le moneytime : rencontre d'anthologie entre Domenico Tedesco et Thorsten Fink
items.summary: Il y avait un duel entre deux entraîneurs bien connus du football belge en Turquie. Domenico Tedesco l'a emporté de toute justesse face à Thorsten Fink.

items.title: Hans Vanaken zag Anderlecht voor duidelijke aanpak kiezen: "Dan is het lastig"
items.summary: Club Brugge speelde vandaag in eigen huis 2-2 gelijk tegen RSC Anderlecht. Een uitslag waar de thuisploeg niet tevreden mee is. Dat blijkt uit de reactie van onder meer Hans Vanaken.

items.title: 🎥 Hallucinante beelden uit Schotland: Old Firm loopt compléét uit de hand
items.summary: In Schotland stond zondag de Old Firm tussen Rangers en Celtic op het programma. Beide teams keken elkaar in de ogen in de beker, in een wedstrijd die na afloop helemaal ontspoorde.

items.title: Henry Lawrence, buteur décisif pour le Standard : les notes des Rouches, vainqueurs sur le fil à Zulte
items.summary: Encore bien trop léger sur le plan offensif, le Standard pensait partager l'enjeu sur la pelouse de Zulte jusqu'au but salvateur de Henry Lawrence dans les dernières minutes.
